description: A2A Part <-> module input/output conversion parity (Algorithm A-PART). The runner calls PartConverter.output_to_parts / parts_to_input directly and partial-matches the result, or asserts the raised error-message substring. parts_to_input raises a plain language-native error (ValueError / Error / Err<String>) WITHOUT a JSON-RPC code; code/Task mapping happens upstream (see notes). A2A 1.0 Part is a flat oneof: {text} | {data} | {raw|url}; output_to_parts returns an Artifact {artifactId: 'art-{taskId|uuid}', parts: [...]} and a DataPart carries NO mediaType.

test_cases.description: Arrays are JSON-serialized into a text Part (json.dumps / JSON.stringify / serde_json), NOT a data Part.

expected_error_message: Message must contain at least one Part
TextPart text is not valid JSON

known_divergences: parts_to_input raises a plain language-native error (Python ValueError, TS Error, Rust Err<String>) with NO JSON-RPC code attached. In the message/send server flow these surface as a FAILED task (a Task already exists), not a top-level -32602 — see skill_resolution.json (unparseable_textpart_yields_failed_task). Only the human-readable message substring is asserted here.

A2A 1.0 DataPart carries no mediaType field; an output dict becomes a flat {data: ...} Part. (docs/spec/test-plan.md TC-PRT-001 mentions part.mediaType; that predates the 1.0 protobuf Part and is not emitted on the wire.)

expected_parts uses the JSON oneof projection ({text}/{data}); each SDK builds its native Part (protobuf oneof in Python/Rust, {content:{$case}} in TS) and the runner compares the projected value. output_to_parts returns an Artifact wrapper with artifactId 'art-{taskId}' (or 'art-{uuid}' when taskId is empty).
